methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor, a Series A chip design company founded by Daniel and Sebastian Schall, is evaluated against a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms. The private peer set includes Etched ($5.0B Series B), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), and Rain AI ($500M Series A).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable method given data constraints. A median across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables yields $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. This reflects material dispersion: Rain AI at the lower bound and Etched at the upper bound. Public-comp anchoring (using 3.0x P/S median of 20.3x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not on file, a common limitation in private company valuation.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $850M to $1.1B, centered at $1.0B. Given Black Semiconductor has raised €254.4M to date, the median implies a post-money valuation consistent with continued investor participation at meaningful dilution. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory and gross margins relative to peers—both critical inputs absent from available data. The wide peer disparity ($500M to $5.0B) reflects stage and technology differentiation within semiconductors, warranting deeper diligence on Black Semiconductor's specific market position and capital efficiency to narrow this band.
